title: Shower steamers
subtitle: Bicarbonate and citric acid tablets that release eucalyptus and peppermint in steam
excerpt: Shower steamers are a bicarbonate of soda and citric acid tablet that fizzes when it contacts water, releasing essential oil vapour in the steam. Unlike bath bombs, they are placed on the shower floor, not dissolved in bathwater. This batch makes 8 to 10 steamers scented with eucalyptus and peppermint.
sourceNotes: Formulators Kitchen shower steamer formulation (formulatorskitchen.com, accessed May 2026): bicarbonate-to-citric-acid ratio and EO rate for shower use. Standard 2:1 bicarb-to-acid ratio from bath fizzy literature.

content.text: A shower steamer uses the same

as a bath bomb, but the essential oil load is higher because the steamer is not submerged in water: the vapour diffuses into the steam rather than diluting into a full bath. Eucalyptus and peppermint are the most commonly used combination for a decongestant, alert-morning effect. Both are toxic to cats; keep steamers away from cats.

during mixing: if humidity triggers the reaction before the mixture is in the mould, the steamer will not fizz properly in the shower. Mix quickly, in a dry bowl, with dry hands, on a dry day. Do not use shower steamers as bath bombs; the essential oil concentration is too high for bathwater skin contact.

items.name: Bicarbonate of soda
items.prepNote: Fine, dry bicarbonate of soda. Sift if it has been stored in a humid cupboard.

items.name: Citric acid
items.prepNote: Fine-ground citric acid. 2:1 ratio of bicarb to citric acid by weight.

items.name: Eucalyptus essential oil
items.prepNote: Eucalyptus globulus. 3.3% of base weight. The primary scent.

items.name: Peppermint essential oil
items.prepNote: 1.7% of base weight. Adds sharpness to the eucalyptus. Toxic to cats.

content.text: Weigh the bicarbonate of soda and citric acid into the dry mixing bowl. Stir briefly to combine. The mixture should look uniform and slightly powdery.

Combine the eucalyptus and peppermint essential oils in a small dish. Drizzle very slowly over the dry mix, stirring constantly. Add only a few drops at a time; adding the oils too quickly can initiate the fizz reaction. Stir for 2 minutes after all the oil is added. The mixture should hold its shape when pressed between the fingers.

Press the mixture firmly into the muffin tin cavities, packing tightly. Smooth the top with the back of a spoon. Leave uncovered at room temperature overnight for the steamers to firm. They will shrink slightly as they cure. Once firm, remove from the tin and wrap each steamer individually in cling film.

Place one steamer on the shower floor at the edge of the spray zone, not directly under the shower head. Enough water to trigger fizzing should reach it via droplets and runoff. One steamer lasts approximately 10 to 15 minutes in a standard shower.

Store individually wrapped in a sealed tin or jar away from humidity. Shelf life is 6 months. Any crumbling or pre-emptive fizzing in storage means the steamers have absorbed atmospheric moisture; they will not perform well in the shower. Discard and remake on a drier day.
